title: Unified run output module
notes:
2026-04-06 14:53 - Task [8l3] complete. Created internal/output/ with RunOutput struct, 3 sink interfaces (BoardSink, CloudSink, RunLogSink), functional options (WithStdout, WithStderr, WithBoard, WithCloud, WithRunLog, WithJSONL), and local IterationResult/RunResult types to avoid import cycles with engine. The RunLogSink interface methods match runlog.Logger's existing method signatures. CloudSink is defined but not yet routed in event methods — wire it when cloud sync is implemented.
2026-04-06 14:56 - Task [wbq] complete. Created BoardSinkAdapter (board_sink.go) and RunLogSinkAdapter (runlog_sink.go) in internal/output/. Key decisions: (1) Added public Server.BroadcastRunStreamEvent() that delegates to the unexported broadcastRunStreamEvent — needed since the adapter lives in a different package. (2) BoardSink interface uses 'any' for status/snap params; the adapter does type assertions to *runrecord.EpicStatus and agent.AgentStateSnapshot, returning nil on wrong type (safe no-op). (3) RunLogSinkAdapter bridges the flat-arg interface (LogIterationEnd, LogRunEnd) to runlog's struct-based methods (IterationEndData, RunEndData). (4) output.go also gained terminal output for context/wave/task events and JSONL support in RunComplete — these came from hook/linter additions, not this task, but were included in the commit since they were already staged.
2026-04-06 14:57 - Task [zal] complete. Terminal formatting was already implemented in output.go by the [wbq] iteration (included in that commit). Added comprehensive test suite: 20+ new tests covering all terminal formatting (context events, task events, RunComplete human/JSONL modes, signal display, JSONL omitempty, progress line padding). Fixed board_sink_test.go build error (TaskID field removed from AgentStateSnapshot). All 40 tests pass.
2026-04-06 15:12 - Task [x51] complete. Replaced all 13 On* callback fields + direct runLog/runRecordStore usage in engine.go with single Output *output.RunOutput field. Key decisions: (1) Expanded RunLogSink interface from 7 to 22 methods to cover all engine logging needs (budget, signals, idle, agent events, context errors). (2) Kept runRecordStore field on Engine solely for wave.Runner construction — only Output routes EpicStatus updates now. (3) Removed SetRunLog/RunLog since runLog field is gone. (4) cmd/tk/cmd/run.go still references the removed On* fields — task [coh] must update run.go to construct RunOutput and set eng.Output instead. (5) ContextFailed vs ContextGenerationFailed: the old code had OnContextFailed for both generation failure and save failure, but runlog had separate LogContextGenerationFailed and LogContextSaveFailed. Added distinct ContextGenerationFailed and ContextSaveFailed methods on RunOutput to preserve this distinction.
2026-04-06 15:21 - Task [coh] complete. Wired RunOutput into run.go and wrapup.go. Key decisions: (1) RunOutput is constructed in runRun() after board/cloud setup but before engine start — outOpts accumulate sinks as they become available, then New(outOpts...) is called once. (2) Runlog sink is added after worktree creation since NewWithWorkDir needs workDir. (3) Removed JSONL field from wrapup.Runner — RunOutput handles JSONL suppression internally. (4) Added CloudInfo, MergeSuccess, PRCreated, WorktreeInfo methods to output.go. (5) WrapupStepResult now uses leading 2-space indent and routes errors to stderr. (6) The 'no ready epics' early-exit JSONL path in runRun() uses inline map since it fires before RunOutput construction. (7) Pre-existing test failure in internal/agent (TestAcpAgent_command/default_gemini_command) is unrelated.
2026-04-06 15:25 - Task [jpw] complete. Added debugf(format, args...) method on Server struct that checks s.devMode before printing. Replaced all 25 [DEBUG] fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) lines with s.debugf(...) calls. Non-debug stderr output (file watcher errors, cloud push errors, shutdown messages, dev mode info) was intentionally left unchanged since those are legitimate warnings/lifecycle messages. The board server is now silent on stderr by default.
